title: Cosmostigma racemosum
taxonomy: Cosmostigma racemosum is a species of plant in the family Achariaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and is classified as a flowering plant. The plant is a member of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the order Malpighiales, Cosmostigma racemosum is further classified as part of the family Achariaceae, along with other related species. This classification system helps to identify and categorize the plant within the broader context of its botanical relationships.

intro: Cosmostigma racemosum, commonly known as Indian Spurge or Spurge, is a plant species belonging to the family Achariaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Cosmostigma racemosum include saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often processed into various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
